content: The Self-Refine framework’s iterative, un-supervised feedback loop can be grounded by multiple-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms to fix a key blind spot of standard Self-Refine: its single-perspective feedback often gets stuck in local optima where the model cannot recognize flaws in its own biased initial output. By inserting self-contrast inconsistent perspective sampling from LLM self-reflection mechanisms into each Self-Refine iteration, we can create a hybrid approach that retains Self-Refine’s advantage of no extra training or labeled data while drastically improving refinement quality.
